summary: Bootstrapping through intermediate capacities (weak→medium→strong) consistently outperforms direct weak→strong supervision across all 7 tested configurations. Average accuracy advantage is 6.4%, driven by error pattern dilution rather than error compounding. Lower weak supervisor accuracy predicts higher bootstrap advantage (r=-0.789).
claims: Staged bootstrapping beats direct weak-to-strong supervision in all 7 tested capability gap configurations
Bootstrap advantage ranges from 2.4% to 10.8% accuracy improvement, averaging 6.4%
Key mechanism is error pattern breaking: bootstrap reduces error overlap from 29.9% to 21.9%
Lower weak supervisor accuracy strongly predicts higher bootstrap advantage (r=-0.789)
Intermediate supervisor quality improves significantly even under weak supervision (+12.5% accuracy)
Bootstrap advantage is highest when weak model is very poor and total capability gap is large

evidence.summary: Raw experimental data: 7 configurations × 5 trials each, comparing direct vs bootstrap supervision
Statistical summary: bootstrap wins 7/7 cases, average advantage 6.4%
Pattern analysis showing correlation between weak accuracy and bootstrap advantage
Detailed gap analysis and error overlap measurements
Main experimental code with synthetic model implementation
Results analysis code for pattern identification

limitations: Synthetic setup only - may not generalize to real LLM training dynamics
No failure cases observed - parameter range may be too narrow
Simplified systematic error model - real errors likely more complex
Small sample size - only 7 configurations tested
Goodhart risk - optimizing for accuracy may miss safety-relevant failure modes
Missing investigation of deception, goal misgeneralization, and other superalignment failure modes
next: Test bootstrapping on realistic language model tasks beyond synthetic classification
Identify failure regimes where bootstrapping hurts rather than helps
Investigate whether results hold for safety-relevant capabilities vs just accuracy
Test on real capability elicitation tasks (e.g., truthfulness, harmlessness)
